title: ONS UK GDP Quarterly National Accounts
short_description: Official quarterly UK GDP estimates by expenditure, income, and output approach from 1955 with regional GVA.
long_description: ONS quarterly national accounts providing estimates of UK Gross Domestic Product (GDP) by three approaches: expenditure (consumption, investment, trade), income (wages, profits, taxes), and output (by industry). Also includes regional Gross Value Added (GVA) at local authority level published annually. Quarterly GDP estimates from 1955. The ONS publishes a preliminary estimate within 28 days of quarter end, revised estimates at 56 and 84 days, and final estimates in the annual Blue Book.
geographic_coverage: United Kingdom (regional GVA for England and Wales)
access_method: Free bulk download; Free API via ONS API
schema_fields: Period|string|Reference quarter e.g. 2024 Q1; GDP_GBP_Millions|decimal|GDP at market prices GBP millions; GDPGrowth_Quarter|decimal|GDP growth quarter-on-quarter %; GDPGrowth_Annual|decimal|GDP growth year-on-year %; Approach|string|Expenditure/Income/Output; SIC_Section|string|Industry section for output approach
tags: GDP, economic growth, national accounts, ONS, UK economy, GVA, quarterly, recession, output
notes: Regional GVA at local authority level published annually in December. ONS API at developer.ons.gov.uk. GDP deflators (price indices) published separately for real terms comparisons.

title: DLUHC Local Authority Revenue and Capital Outturn
short_description: Annual local authority spending and revenue statistics for all English councils covering income, expenditure, and reserves.
long_description: DLUHC publishes annual Revenue Outturn (RO) and Capital Outturn (CO) statistics for all English local authorities covering total spending, service-level expenditure, grant income, council tax income, borrowing, and usable reserves. Covers 319 billing authorities and over 400 precepting authorities. Data collected via the Revenue Account Budget/Outturn return. Essential for council financial analysis, fiscal stress modelling, and public finance research.
provider: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government (MHCLG)
record_count: ~400 local authorities x 50+ service lines x annual
schema_fields: LA_Code|string|ONS local authority code; LA_Name|string|Local authority name; Year|integer|Financial year; Service|string|Service category e.g. Education/Social Care/Highways; GrossExpenditure|decimal|Gross expenditure GBP thousands; GrantIncome|decimal|Grant income GBP thousands; CouncilTaxIncome|decimal|Council tax income GBP thousands; UsableReserves|decimal|Total usable reserves GBP thousands
tags: local authority, council, spending, finance, budget, DLUHC, council tax, reserves, public finance
notes: Revenue Outturn (RO) and Capital Outturn (CO) published separately. Section 114 notices (financial emergency) tracked separately via DLUHC. Scotland, Wales, and NI publish separate local government finance statistics.

title: London Ambulance Service Incident Data
short_description: Annual London Ambulance Service call and response data by incident category and borough including response times.
long_description: The London Ambulance Service (LAS) publishes data on emergency calls responded to across Greater London, including incident categories (Category 1: life-threatening to Category 4: non-urgent), response times against national standards, and borough-level demand. Also publishes mental health-related call data. Category 1 target is 7 minutes mean response; Category 2 is 18 minutes mean. Published on the London Datastore. Useful for healthcare planning and emergency services research.
schema_fields: Borough|string|London borough; Year|integer|Reference year; Category|string|Incident category 1-4; Incidents|integer|Total incidents attended; MeanResponseTime_Minutes|decimal|Mean response time in minutes; PercentWithinTarget|decimal|Percentage meeting response time target

notes: Mental health incident data published separately on London Datastore. National ambulance response time data available via NHS England. LAS also publishes OHCA (out of hospital cardiac arrest) survival data.

title: ONS Small Area Income Estimates
short_description: Annual net household income and income inequality estimates for all Middle Super Output Areas in England and Wales.
long_description: ONS model-based estimates of total annual net household income (before and after housing costs) and median and mean income for all Middle Super Output Areas (MSOAs) in England and Wales, approximately 7,200 geographies averaging 8,000 residents each. Also includes Gini coefficient and income quintile share ratios as measures of inequality. Data from financial year 2002/03. Published annually. Used for deprivation analysis, retail catchment modelling, and planning.
schema_fields: MSOA_Code|string|ONS MSOA code; MSOA_Name|string|MSOA name; Total_Annual_Income_GBP|decimal|Total net annual household income GBP; Median_Income_GBP|decimal|Median net annual household income GBP; Mean_Income_GBP|decimal|Mean net annual household income GBP; Gini_Coefficient|decimal|Income inequality measure 0-1
tags: income, inequality, household, MSOA, small area, earnings, England, Wales, deprivation, ONS
notes: Model-based estimates — not direct survey measurements for small areas. Linked to Indices of Multiple Deprivation (IMD). Used for retail demand modelling and planning.

title: MHCLG English Indices of Multiple Deprivation
short_description: Official deprivation rankings for all 32,844 Lower Super Output Areas in England across 7 deprivation domains.
long_description: The English Indices of Multiple Deprivation (IMD) are the official measure of relative deprivation for Lower Super Output Areas (LSOAs) in England. The IMD combines 39 indicators across 7 domains: Income, Employment, Education, Health, Crime, Barriers to Housing and Services, and Living Environment. Each LSOA is ranked from 1 (most deprived) to 32,844 (least deprived). Published approximately every 4-5 years; latest edition is IMD 2019. Widely used in public health, planning, and policy research.
provider: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government (MHCLG)
schema_fields: LSOA_Code|string|ONS LSOA code; LSOA_Name|string|LSOA name; IMD_Score|decimal|Overall deprivation score; IMD_Rank|integer|Rank 1=most deprived; IMD_Decile|integer|Decile 1=10% most deprived; Income_Score|decimal|Income domain score; Employment_Score|decimal|Employment domain score; Education_Score|decimal|Education domain score; Health_Score|decimal|Health deprivation score; Crime_Score|decimal|Crime domain score
tags: deprivation, IMD, LSOA, poverty, health, income, employment, education, England, inequality
notes: IMD 2019 is the latest. IMD 2025 expected. Scotland, Wales, and NI publish separate deprivation indices. Domain scores allow analysis of specific types of deprivation.

title: HMRC PAYE Real Time Information Employment Statistics
short_description: Monthly employee and median earnings estimates from PAYE RTI data by local authority, industry, and age group.
long_description: Experimental HMRC statistics derived from Pay As You Earn Real Time Information (PAYE RTI) employer returns. Provides monthly estimates of the number of employees and median monthly earnings by local authority, Standard Industrial Classification section, and age group. Available from April 2014. More timely than ASHE (available monthly vs annually) but experimental status — methodology still evolving. Published by ONS in collaboration with HMRC.
provider: HM Revenue & Customs / Office for National Statistics

tags: employment, earnings, PAYE, HMRC, RTI, monthly, local authority, industry, wages, UK
notes: Experimental statistics — approach may be revised. PAYE data covers employees only; self-employed not included. More timely than ASHE for local area monitoring.

title: GLA London Development Database
short_description: Records of all major planning permissions and completions in London since 2004 including housing mix, tenure, and developer details.
long_description: The London Development Database (LDD) is the GLA's annual monitoring dataset of all major planning permissions (10+ dwellings or 1,000+ sqm of non-residential floorspace) in Greater London. Submitted by all 33 London boroughs. Contains planning application details, development type, site address, proposed uses, number of units, tenure mix (market/affordable/social rent), developer, and development status. Covers permissions from 2004 and completions from 2004. Essential for housing supply monitoring and planning research.

Data available at national, regional, local authority, ward, MSOA, LSOA, and Output Area levels. Microdata available via Secure Research Service. Over 8,000 datasets across 320+ topics.

title: UK Biobank Research Resource
short_description: Longitudinal health and genetic data for 500,000 UK participants aged 40-69 with linked hospital and primary care records.
long_description: UK Biobank is a large-scale biomedical database containing genetic, health, and lifestyle information from 500,000 UK participants recruited between 2006-2010. Includes self-reported questionnaire data, physical measurements, cognitive tests, imaging data, genetic array data, and linked electronic health records (NHS hospital, cancer registry, death records, primary care). The world's most powerful health resource for research into common diseases. Access requires application and approval.

title: Companies House Register of Overseas Entities
short_description: Register of all overseas entities owning UK property — launched 2022 under the Economic Crime Act with beneficial owner details.
long_description: The Register of Overseas Entities (ROE) was launched by Companies House in August 2022 under the Economic Crime (Transparency and Enforcement) Act 2022. All overseas legal entities that own UK land or property must register and disclose their beneficial owners. The register contains overseas entity name, country of incorporation, registered office, nature of control, and beneficial owner details. Essential for anti-money laundering analysis and property ownership transparency. Linked to HMLR overseas company data.

title: TfL Road Network TLRN Performance Data
short_description: Traffic flow, journey time, and reliability statistics for the Transport for London Road Network (TLRN) by corridor.
long_description: TfL publishes data on the performance of the Transport for London Road Network (TLRN) — the 580km strategic road network in London managed by TfL. Includes average journey times, vehicle flow counts, network delay, and journey time reliability by time of day and corridor. Also includes annual statistics on road casualties on the TLRN. Key dataset for understanding road congestion patterns in central and inner London.

title: ONS Foreign Direct Investment Statistics
short_description: Annual UK inward and outward foreign direct investment by country, industry, and component from 1984.
long_description: ONS annual statistics on UK Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) covering both inward investment (into UK) and outward investment (from UK abroad), broken down by investing country, industry, and component (equity, debt instruments, reinvested earnings). Includes stocks and flows. UK FDI data submitted to the IMF and OECD. London attracts approximately 70% of UK inward FDI. Available from 1984. Used for economic development policy, investor relations, and economic research.

title: ONS Business Register and Employment Survey (BRES)

Based on the Inter-Departmental Business Register (IDBR) covering VAT and PAYE-registered businesses. Provides both full-time and part-time employment estimates. title: GLA Land Use Survey

Uses Ordnance Survey MasterMap as the base geometry and classifies land into residential, commercial, industrial, transport, open space, community/education/health, and vacant categories. Updated approximately every 5 years using combination of satellite imagery, planning data, and field surveys. Covers the entire 1,572 km² of Greater London. Essential for planning, housing capacity, and land availability studies.

notes: GeoPackage format. Large file size. Used in GLA's Strategic Land Availability Assessment (SLAA). OS MasterMap TOID links to Ordnance Survey base data.

title: HMCTS Court Statistics — Civil, Family, and Criminal
short_description: Quarterly court statistics for all HM Courts and Tribunals Service courts in England and Wales covering cases, hearings, and outcomes.
long_description: HM Courts and Tribunals Service (HMCTS) publishes quarterly statistics on civil, family, criminal, and tribunal proceedings in England and Wales. Civil covers claims, defences, judgments. Family covers divorce petitions, injunctions, care proceedings. Criminal covers prosecutions, convictions, custodial sentences by offence. Employment tribunal statistics also published. Data from 1990s. Used for legal sector analysis, access to justice research, and commercial litigation benchmarking.
schema_fields: Period|string|Reference quarter; CourtType|string|Crown Court/Magistrates/Civil/Family; Region|string|HMCTS region; CaseType|string|Category of proceeding; FiledCases|integer|Cases filed in period; HearingsConducted|integer|Hearings held; OutcomeType|string|Conviction/Acquittal/Judgment; SentenceType|string|Custodial/Suspended/Fine if criminal

tags: courts, criminal, civil, family, HMCTS, justice, crime, sentencing, England, Wales, legal
notes: Separate publications for employment tribunals. Crown Court detailed data available with offence codes. Linked to Home Office criminal justice statistics for full CJS pipeline.

title: Zoopla UK Property Market Listings Data
short_description: Current and historical residential property listings across the UK with asking price, bedrooms, EPC rating, and agent details — via scraping.
long_description: Zoopla.co.uk is one of the UK's largest property listing portals, publishing current for-sale and for-rent listings across all UK areas. Each listing includes asking price, property type, number of bedrooms, EPC rating, estate agent, local area information, and listing date. Requires scraping via structured HTML or the Zoopla API (now restricted to partners). Historical listing data can be captured over time to build a time-series of asking price trends and stock levels. Useful for property market intelligence, competitor analysis, and pricing models.
licence: Commercial — scraping for commercial purposes requires agreement with Zoopla
record_count: ~1 million active listings at any one time
date_coverage: Current and recent listings; historical archive requires ongoing capture
access_method: Web scraping — terms of service review required; commercial API for partners

title: LinkedIn UK Job Postings via Data Extraction
short_description: UK job vacancy postings including job title, employer, salary, skills required, and location — via LinkedIn scraping or data partners.
long_description: LinkedIn is the largest professional job posting platform in the UK with millions of live UK job listings. Each posting contains job title, employer name, location (city/postcode), employment type, salary range (where disclosed), required skills, seniority level, and date posted. LinkedIn publishes aggregate labour market insights (via LinkedIn Economic Graph) but individual job listings require data partner agreements or scraping. A valuable signal for real-time labour market demand, skills requirements, and emerging roles.
licence: Commercial — LinkedIn User Agreement prohibits scraping; data partner agreements required
format: HTML (scraping restricted); JSON via LinkedIn API (approved partners)
record_count: ~1 million UK active job postings at any time
date_coverage: Real-time; historical data limited without ongoing capture
access_method: Negotiated licence — LinkedIn Data Partner programme; LinkedIn Economic Graph for research access
schema_fields: job_id|string|LinkedIn job posting ID; title|string|Job title; company_name|string|Employer name; location|string|Job location city; employment_type|string|Full-time/Part-time/Contract; seniority_level|string|Entry/Mid-Senior/Director; salary_min|decimal|Minimum salary GBP if disclosed; salary_max|decimal|Maximum salary GBP if disclosed; skills|array|Required skills list; posted_date|date|Date posting was published

notes: FORMAT: LinkedIn strictly enforces anti-scraping. Research access via LinkedIn Economic Graph at economicgraph.linkedin.com. Alternative: use Indeed or Reed job APIs which are more accessible. Burning Glass/Lightcast provide commercial job postings datasets.

title: Land Registry Title Register (HMLR) — Property Ownership
short_description: Complete register of all property titles in England and Wales showing current owner, tenure, mortgage, and purchase price.
long_description: The HM Land Registry Title Register contains over 26 million registered titles in England and Wales. Each title includes the registered proprietor (owner), tenure (freehold/leasehold), any charges (mortgages), price paid if registered after 2000, and full property description including plan reference. Individual title register access costs £3 per title (online). Bulk access via the HMLR Use Land and Property Data service for commercial users. The most authoritative record of UK property ownership.
licence: Commercial — licence required for bulk access; individual titles available for £3 each
format: CSV bulk (registered proprietor name and address); Individual PDF (full title register)
date_coverage: Continuous from 1990 for most areas; some titles older
access_method: Paid bulk download via Use Land and Property Data (HMLR commercial service); £3 per title for individual access
schema_fields: Title_Number|string|HMLR title number; Tenure|string|Freehold/Leasehold; Property_Address|string|Full address of registered property; Proprietor_Name|string|Registered owner name; Proprietor_Category|string|Private individual/Corporate body/Overseas company; Price_Paid|decimal|Price paid GBP if registered after 2000; Charges|boolean|Whether charge registered against title

tags: property ownership, land registry, titles, mortgages, proprietor, England, Wales, freehold, leasehold
notes: Registered Proprietor Name and Address dataset available as bulk download for commercial users — enquire at use-land-property-data.service.gov.uk. Company Name and Address dataset also available. Individual title register: gov.uk/search-property-information-land-registry.

title: Financial Ombudsman Service Annual Complaints Data
short_description: Annual complaints data to the Financial Ombudsman Service by financial product, firm name, and outcome.
long_description: The Financial Ombudsman Service (FOS) publishes biannual and annual data on complaints received and resolved, broken down by financial product (PPI, mortgages, car finance, investments, insurance, banking), firm name, and decision outcome (upheld/not upheld by FOS). Over 2,000 named financial firms included. Time series from 2010. Essential for consumer protection research, firm reputational analysis, and financial sector conduct analysis. PPI complaints dominated until 2019 deadline.
record_count: ~2,000 firms x 20 product types x annual
schema_fields: Period|string|Reference half-year e.g. H1 2024; FirmName|string|Financial firm name; FirmFCA_Ref|string|FCA reference number; ProductGroup|string|Financial product category; ComplaintsReceived|integer|FOS complaints received; ComplaintsResolved|integer|Complaints resolved in period; UpheldByFOS_Pct|decimal|Percentage upheld in consumer favour

tags: FOS, complaints, financial ombudsman, consumer protection, banking, insurance, PPI, conduct, UK
notes: PPI complaints closed August 2019. Car finance (commission) complaints surge since 2024 court ruling. FCA also publishes firm-level complaints data separately under FCA complaints return.

title: UK Company Insolvency Filings from Companies House (Dissolved)
short_description: Bulk dataset of all dissolved and struck-off UK companies with dissolution dates, reasons, and officer history.
long_description: Companies House publishes bulk data on all dissolved and struck-off companies, including the reason for dissolution (voluntary strike-off, compulsory strike-off, dissolution following liquidation). Contains company name, number, incorporation date, dissolution date, company type, and last registered address. Over 10 million dissolved company records. Linked to Companies House main data via company number. Essential for business failure analysis, credit risk, and economic research.
schema_fields: CompanyName|string|Company name at dissolution; CompanyNumber|string|Unique company number; CompanyCategory|string|Company legal type; CompanyStatus|string|Dissolved/Converted-Closed; DissolutionDate|date|Date of dissolution; IncorporationDate|date|Date of incorporation; LastRegisteredAddress|string|Address at time of dissolution

tags: dissolved companies, strike-off, insolvency, Companies House, business failure, liquidation, UK
notes: Download via companieshouse.gov.uk bulk products page. Dissolved companies not in the live company snapshot — requires separate download. Useful for time-series analysis of company formation and failure rates.

title: GLA London Datastore Economic Indicators
short_description: Monthly and quarterly economic indicator dashboard for London covering employment, output, wages, confidence, and housing.
long_description: The GLA Economics team publishes monthly Labour Market Updates and quarterly economic indicator dashboards for London, drawing on ONS Labour Force Survey, PAYE RTI, HMRC VAT data, and GLA modelling. Covers London unemployment rate, employment rate, average earnings, business confidence (via GfK), consumer spending, and housing indicators. Over 100 spreadsheets archived. Essential for London economic monitoring and policymaking.

title: Ofsted Early Years Provider Inspection Reports
short_description: Inspection outcomes for all Ofsted-registered early years providers in England including nurseries and childminders.
long_description: Ofsted publishes inspection outcomes for all registered early years providers in England: nurseries, childminders, holiday clubs, and other childcare providers. Approximately 70,000 registered providers. Data includes overall effectiveness rating, sub-judgements for quality of education, behaviour, personal development, and leadership, as well as registration status and number of places. Updated monthly. Separate from the school inspections dataset. Essential for childcare market analysis and local authority sufficiency assessments.

title: Global Financial Centres Index (GFCI)
short_description: Biannual ranking of 120 global financial centres by competitiveness across business environment, human capital, and infrastructure.
long_description: The Global Financial Centres Index (GFCI) is a biannual survey-based ranking of financial centre competitiveness published by Z/Yen Partners and the China Development Institute. Covers 120 financial centres across five dimensions: Business Environment, Human Capital, Infrastructure, Financial Sector Development, and Reputation and General. London and New York consistently compete for 1st and 2nd place. Widely cited by policymakers, financial institutions, and media. Published since 2007.

title: Ofgem Electricity and Gas Supplier Market Share
short_description: Annual and quarterly energy supplier market shares for domestic electricity and gas customers in Great Britain.
long_description: Ofgem publishes quarterly and annual data on the market share of domestic electricity and gas suppliers in Great Britain by number of customer accounts. Covers all licensed suppliers including the Big 6 (British Gas, EDF, EON, Scottish Power, Ovo, Npower/E.ON) and challenger suppliers. Data from 2013. Also includes smart meter installation progress by supplier. Used for energy market competition analysis and consumer protection research.

title: GLA London Employment by Detailed Industry (4-digit SIC)
short_description: Annual employee job estimates for London down to 4-digit SIC level from 1998 covering all boroughs and major sectors.
long_description: GLA Economics publishes annual estimates of employee jobs in London at 4-digit Standard Industry Classification (SIC 2007) level, covering the period from 1998. Based on ONS BRES and Workforce Jobs (WFJ) data. Provides borough-level and London-wide breakdowns. Covers approximately 800 industry codes at 4-digit level, enabling detailed analysis of London's financial services, creative industries, tech, professional services, and retail sectors. Largest and most detailed London sectoral employment dataset.
record_count: ~800 SIC codes x 33 boroughs x 25+ years

title: UK Intellectual Property Office Patent and Trade Mark Statistics
short_description: Annual and quarterly UK patent applications, grants, and trade mark registrations filed at the UK IPO since 1990.
long_description: The Intellectual Property Office (IPO) publishes statistics on UK patent applications, patent grants, trade mark applications and registrations, design applications, and copyright. Includes breakdowns by technology sector, applicant nationality, and UK regions. International patents (PCT) and European patents (EPO) designating UK also covered. Data from 1990 for patents; 2000 for trade marks. Key source for innovation analysis, R&D benchmarking, and IP strategy research.

title: HESA Higher Education Statistics — Students and Staff
short_description: Annual higher education statistics for all UK universities covering student enrolments, staff, finances, and graduate outcomes.
long_description: The Higher Education Statistics Agency (HESA) publishes annual statistics on all UK higher education providers. Covers student enrolments by subject, level, mode, nationality, and institution; staff headcount and pay; institutional income and expenditure; and graduate outcomes (employment and earnings after study). Over 300 HE providers. Data from 1994. The Graduate Outcomes survey links graduates to earnings data from HMRC. Essential for HE sector analysis, student recruitment, and policy research.

title: ONS UK National Travel Survey
short_description: Annual household travel survey for England covering trips, distances, modes, journey purposes, and car ownership since 1965.
long_description: The National Travel Survey (NTS) is an annual household survey for England providing detailed information on travel behaviour: number of trips, distances, modes of transport, journey purposes, and travel time. Covers private car, public transport, walking, and cycling. Sample of approximately 14,000 households. Annual data from 1965 (some breaks). Special interest tables published for London. Microdata available via UK Data Service. The primary source for modal share and travel demand statistics.
licence: Open Government Licence v3.0 (aggregates); UK Data Service licence (microdata)
access_method: Free aggregates at gov.uk; Microdata via UK Data Service

title: GLA Tech Nation / London Tech Ecosystem Data
short_description: Annual analysis of the UK tech ecosystem covering VC investment, startup counts, tech jobs, and digital skills by city and sector.
long_description: Tech Nation (formerly compiled by Tech Nation, now by DSIT and Dealroom) publishes annual statistics on the UK and London tech ecosystem. Covers venture capital investment by city, sector, and stage; number of tech startups and scaleups; tech job vacancies; digital skills demand; and London's position in global tech centre rankings. Dealroom provides the underlying company and investment data. London receives approximately 50% of all UK tech VC investment. Published annually since 2015.
